articles.title: WA AG explains why the state is trying to ban Kalshi and other predictive markets
articles.summary: Washington's Attorney General is making the case for why the state wants to shut down Kalshi and other prediction markets operating there. This could set a precedent for other states to follow suit, potentially fragmenting the US prediction market landscape just as it was gaining mainstream traction.

articles.title: Exclusive | White House Warns Staff Not To Place Bets on Prediction Markets Amid Iran War
articles.summary: The White House has reportedly issued internal warnings prohibiting staff from betting on prediction markets related to potential conflict with Iran. The move signals growing official recognition that prediction markets have become influential enough to warrant formal restrictions on government insiders who might possess material non-public information.

articles.title: Judge rejects bid to stop Arizona’s prosecution of Kalshi on wagering charges
articles.summary: Arizona can continue its criminal prosecution of prediction market Kalshi after a federal judge rejected the company's attempt to halt the case. The ruling keeps alive one of the biggest legal threats facing regulated prediction markets, potentially setting precedent for how states can crack down on political betting even when federally approved.

articles.title: Bill to make prediction markets illegal in MN clears first Senate Committee
articles.summary: Minnesota's Senate committee just advanced legislation that would ban prediction markets in the state. If it passes, Minnesota could become the first state to explicitly criminalize prediction market participation, potentially setting a precedent for other states to follow suit.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Turned the World Into a Casino
articles.summary: Prediction markets have exploded beyond politics and finance into everything from celebrity deaths to weather patterns, creating a global betting ecosystem where literally anything can become a tradeable outcome. This massive expansion means more liquidity and opportunities for sharp bettors, but also attracts casual gamblers who don't understand the fundamentals, potentially creating mispriced markets ripe for exploitation.

articles.title: Minn. lawmakers look to rein in prediction markets, citing concerns about ethics, addiction
articles.summary: Minnesota lawmakers are pushing legislation to restrict prediction markets over worries about gambling addiction and ethical concerns. The move signals growing regulatory pressure that could spread to other states and force platforms to navigate a patchwork of conflicting rules across jurisdictions.

articles.title: Bank of America Sees a $1.1 Trillion Market for Sports Event Betting in US
articles.summary: Bank of America analysts project the US sports betting market could reach $1.1 trillion in total handle, a massive expansion from current levels that signals the industry's explosive growth potential. This validates what prediction market operators already know: Americans have an enormous appetite for putting money behind their opinions, and sports betting's mainstream acceptance creates a pathway for more sophisticated forecasting markets to follow.

articles.title: WATCH: Kalshi founders talk insider trading, gambling, Don Jr. on "The Axios Show"
articles.summary: Kalshi's founders appeared on "The Axios Show" to address controversies around insider trading allegations, gambling regulations, and Donald Trump Jr.'s involvement with the platform. The interview likely signals Kalshi's push to legitimize prediction markets amid regulatory scrutiny and public skepticism about whether these platforms are sophisticated forecasting tools or glorified betting sites.

articles.title: One of Kalshi's fastest-growing markets is about tech layoffs, cofounders say
articles.summary: Kalshi's cofounders report that tech layoff prediction markets have become one of their platform's fastest-growing categories. The surge shows bettors are hungry for ways to profit from corporate carnage, turning workplace anxiety into tradeable assets.

articles.title: Insider Trading in Prediction Markets Poses Compliance Risks
articles.summary: Prediction markets are wrestling with insider trading scandals as traders with privileged information skew odds and undermine market integrity. Regulators are now eyeing these platforms more closely, which could mean stricter rules and compliance costs that change how prediction markets operate.

articles.title: Newly created Polymarket accounts bet big on US-Iran ceasefire in hours before Trump’s announcement
articles.summary: Several brand-new Polymarket accounts placed large wagers on a US-Iran ceasefire just hours before Trump announced exactly that outcome. The timing screams insider trading, highlighting how prediction markets remain vulnerable to information asymmetries despite their reputation for aggregating public wisdom.

articles.title: Polymarket and Kalshi: the new gods of the attention economy
articles.summary: Polymarket and Kalshi have become the new kingmakers of media attention, with their odds driving news cycles and shaping public discourse around everything from elections to pop culture. When prediction markets start dictating what journalists write about and what politicians respond to, they've transcended simple betting platforms to become genuine information infrastructure.
